methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $42M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Layup Parts is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Zack Eakin that has raised $2M+ to date. The valuation universe spans nine peers across robotics and manufacturing—one public company (277810.KQ) and eight private firms including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity—though same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within this sector remain limited.

The Stage-Progression method is the applicable triangulation approach, using the company's $2M+ raised as a $42M total-raised proxy and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage transitions in venture capital. This yields an implied valuation of $105M. A peer-median approach could not be reliably applied due to insufficient narrow peer comparables at the same stage and valuation tier within robotics and manufacturing; while seven same-sector peers exist, none met the threshold of three or more datapoints in filtered subsets, making sector-wide medians misleading.

The single-method analysis supports a valuation range of $89M to $121M, with a median estimate of $105M. Key uncertainties include the limited peer set at this stage and the reliance on a single triangulation method. The $42M proxy itself warrants validation against actual deployment across product development, go-to-market, and working capital. Further clarity on cash burn, revenue traction, and customer concentration would refine the range materially.
